title: Bone Deep
synopsis: Diabetic foot infection with suspected osteomyelitis and bacteremia in a patient with CKD Stage 3b and a low-risk penicillin allergy history.

timelineStages.label: T0
timelineStages.title: Admission
timelineStages.description: Empiric therapy must begin before culture results are available.
timelineStages.defaultFeedback: Select empiric antimicrobial therapy. Consider spectrum, renal function, and allergy history.

timelineStages.label: T12
timelineStages.title: 12 Hours
timelineStages.description: MRI confirms osteomyelitis. Renal function has worsened.

timelineStages.label: T36
timelineStages.title: 36–48 Hours
timelineStages.description: Blood culture results finalized. Reassess and de-escalate.

timelineStages.label: Outcome
timelineStages.title: Clinical Outcome
timelineStages.description: Treatment course assessment based on stewardship decisions.

t0.title: Presenting Syndrome
t0.summary: Diabetic foot infection with suspected osteomyelitis and bacteremia
t0.detail: Blood cultures drawn ×2. Plain film: periosteal reaction at 5th metatarsal. MRI pending. Wound with purulent drainage; bone visible at base.

t0.title: Admission Labs
t0.detail: Estimated CrCl ~32 mL/min (CKD 3b baseline). CRP elevated. Blood cultures pending.

t12.title: MRI Right Foot
t12.detail: MRI confirms osteomyelitis of the 5th metatarsal head with adjacent soft tissue abscess. Podiatry consulted; debridement scheduled.

t12.title: Worsening Renal Function
t12.detail: AKI on CKD. Nephrotoxic agents require dose reassessment. Vancomycin AUC monitoring indicated if on board.

t12.title: AKI Alert
t12.summary: Acute kidney injury — review renally cleared agents
t12.detail: CrCl now below 30. Cefepime, vancomycin, piperacillin-tazobactam, and daptomycin all require adjustment.

t36.title: Blood Culture Final
t36.detail: Blood cultures ×2 both bottles positive. Oxacillin susceptible. Interpretation: Methicillin-Susceptible Staphylococcus aureus. De-escalation to beta-lactam preferred.

t36.title: Clinical Status
t36.detail: Inflammatory markers trending down. Foot erythema decreased. Ready for targeted therapy de-escalation.

outcome.title: Treatment Course Summary
outcome.detail: Review regimen choices across the timeline. Outcome reflects coverage, safety, de-escalation, and renal stewardship.

vancomycin+cefepime.message: Good empiric coverage. Vancomycin + cefepime avoids vanco/pip-tazo nephrotoxicity interaction. Ensure cefepime renal dose adjustment at CrCl ~30.

vancomycin+piperacillin_tazobactam.message: Broad coverage but vancomycin + pip-tazo carries significant nephrotoxicity signal — especially concerning with baseline CKD and worsening AKI.

vancomycin.message: Incomplete gram-negative coverage for diabetic foot infection. MRSA coverage alone is a gap empirically.

daptomycin+cefepime.message: Valid alternative. Obtain baseline CK; daptomycin inactivated in pneumonia (not applicable here).

cefazolin.message: No MRSA coverage empirically. Cefazolin is optimal once MSSA confirmed — not before.

meropenem+vancomycin.message: Excessive empiric spectrum without documented resistant organism risk. Carbapenem stewardship failure.

linezolid.message: Bacteriostatic agent inappropriate as monotherapy for empiric bacteremia. No gram-negative coverage.

daptomycin.message: Gram-positive coverage only. Diabetic foot infections typically require empiric gram-negative coverage.

default.message: Review spectrum against syndrome, renal function, and allergy history.

cefazolin.message: Optimal MSSA de-escalation. Beta-lactams superior to vancomycin for MSSA bacteremia. Low-risk penicillin allergy supports cephalosporin use.

vancomycin.message: Continuing vancomycin for confirmed MSSA is a stewardship failure. Susceptibility ≠ optimal agent selection.

nafcillin.message: Active for MSSA but higher hepatotoxicity burden than cefazolin. Acknowledge penicillin allergy history.

daptomycin.message: Acceptable when beta-lactam truly contraindicated. Low-risk allergy history does not justify avoidance.

linezolid.message: Bacteriostatic — not appropriate for Staphylococcal bacteremia. High treatment failure risk.

default.message: Targeted therapy should match culture results and clinical improvement.
